bids.reasoning: The negotiation has just started, and the opponent's first offer provides very low utility for me across all issues. Given the early stage of the negotiation (t=0.0), I will respond with my optimal opening offer to strongly anchor the negotiation and signal my ideal outcomes, focusing on the highest utility values for all issues.

The negotiation is in its very early stages (t=0.02). The opponent has proposed the exact same offer both times, which yields extremely low utility for me across all issues. To show a willingness to negotiate while protecting my heavily weighted issues (issueA, issueB, and issueC), I will make a tiny concession on issueE (my lowest priority) to see how the opponent responds.

The negotiation has just started (t=0.04) and the opponent is holding completely firm on a very low-utility offer for me (all valueE). Since it is still early, I will maintain my high-utility values on my most important issues (issueA, issueB, issueC) but make a minor concession on issueD to explore whether the opponent is willing to move from their opening stance. By offering valueC for issueD, I maintain an overall utility above 0.95 while signaling flexibility.

It is very early in the negotiation (t=0.06) and the opponent is repeatedly bidding identical offers (valueE across all issues) without making any concessions. I am maintaining my high-utility position on my most critical issues (A, B, and C) while making a minor concession on my lowest-weighted issues (D and E) to see if the opponent is willing to reciprocate.

The current time fraction is very early (t=0.08), but the opponent is repeatedly sending the exact same low-utility offer across all issues (`valueE` globally). To encourage them to participate in real negotiation without compromising my position significantly, I will continue making slight exploratory concessions on my lowest-weighted issues (such as `issueD`), while standing firm on my highest-priority issues (`issueA`, `issueB`, and `issueC`) to ensure my total utility remains securely above 0.85.

The opponent has repeatedly sent the exact same offer without any concessions, suggesting a rigid early-game strategy. Since the time fraction is still very low (0.1), I will maintain a high-utility posture but strategically concede by matching their requested value on my least important issue (issueE) to signal cooperation. I will hold firm on my most preferred values for my high-weight issues (issueA, issueB, and issueC) to protect my overall utility.

The opponent is repeating the exact same offer (all valueE) with no concessions, which provides very low utility for me (around 0.25). Since the current time fraction is very low (t=0.12), I will maintain a high-utility position but make a targeted concession. I will offer the opponent's preferred value for my least important issue where I haven't tried conceding yet (issueD: valueE) while keeping my top preferred values on all other issues, seeing if this yields a counter-offer with some movement.

The opponent has repeatedly proposed `valueE` for all issues without making any concessions. Since it is still early in the negotiation (t=0.14), I will maintain my highest utility requests for my most important issues (A, B, and C) while conceding to the opponent's exact requested `valueE` for my two lowest-importance issues (D and E). This targeted tradeoff maintains a strong overall utility for me (~0.77) while extending a clear olive branch on the issues that matter least to my bottom line.

The opponent continues to repeat their initial offer (`valueE` across the board), which holds very low utility (0.25) for me. Since we are still early in the negotiation (t=0.16), I will make a minor concession by offering `valueB` on `issueB`, which maintains my overall utility above 0.9. This tests whether the opponent is willing to accept slightly different value profiles before determining more drastic trade-offs.

The current time is early (t=0.18), and the opponent is completely unrelenting, stubbornly repeating the exact same offer (all valueE) which gives me my minimal reservation value possible. I will maintain a firm stance on my most critical issues (issueA, issueC) while making slight exploratory concessions on others (like issueB) to see if they respond to any variation. I aim to keep my total utility around 0.8 until we reach the middle stages of the negotiation.

The opponent has repeatedly proposed the identical offer of 'valueE' for all issues throughout the negotiation, showing no willingness to concede yet. As the deadline is still far away at t = 0.20, I will maintain my highest-priority items (issueA) and continue making exploratory concessions on other issues like issueC and issueD. I am aiming to identify alternative values that the opponent might find acceptable while maintaining my utility above 0.75.

The negotiation is still in the early stages (t=0.22), and the opponent has consistently repeated their opening offer with no concessions, yielding very low utility for me. Therefore, I will maintain a high-utility posture while slightly varying the values on less critical issues like issueC. This signals continued willingness to explore the space while protecting my top priorities.

The opponent is remaining completely rigid, repeatedly proposing the same offer of `valueE` for all issues which provides extremely low utility to me. Since we are still early in the negotiation (t=0.24), I will maintain a firm stance on my high-priority issues (issueA, issueB, and issueC) while slightly shifting my proposal on my lower-priority issues (issueD and issueE) to explore if the opponent will find other combinations acceptable.

The time fraction is 0.26, still early in the negotiation. The opponent is stubbornly proposing only the `{issueA: valueE, issueB: valueE, issueC: valueE, issueD: valueE, issueE: valueE}` package, which grants me very poor utility (~0.25). Being early, I will maintain high utility (>0.75) by holding my top preferences for issues A, B, and C, but I will offer `valueE` for `issueE` and a different high-utility value for `issueD` to probe whether they are at least willing to accept their values on the issues with the lowest weight to me.

The opponent continues to stubbornly request 'valueE' across all issues without any concessions, ignoring all attempts to compromise. As the time fraction is still relatively early (0.28), I will explore their flexibility by offering 'valueE' on issueC, an issue of medium importance to me, while retaining my top preferences for my high priority issues. This maintains my utility around 0.77 while seeing if they will break from their rigid opening stance.

The opponent has rigidly maintained the exact same offer (all valueE), which unfortunately provides me with very low utility. Because the time fraction is still only 0.30, I will not make drastic concessions. Instead, I will continue proposing high-utility bids, this time varying issues B and E to explore potential areas of compromise while keeping my core issues (A, C, and D) at their maximum utilities.

The opponent's offers have been completely rigid, stubbornly demanding valueE across all issues. As time progresses towards the mid-game, I will start exploring concessions on my high-weight issues to probe if the opponent is willing to move. I will offer valueB on issueA while keeping my top preferences elsewhere, targeting an overall utility around 0.8.

The opponent has stubbornly persisted with the exact same offer (valueE for all issues) for 18 consecutive rounds without moving. To provoke some meaningful exchange, I will concede completely to their requested 'valueE' on my two lowest-priority issues (issueD and issueE) and make a calculated concession on issueB. This brings my utility target to a still-comfortable 0.70 while directly accommodating their stated preferences on multiple issues. I will hold firm on issueA and issueC where my utility is heavily concentrated.

The opponent has stubbornly refused to budge from their opening offer, repeating the exact same bid for 18 consecutive turns. Given that we are still in the early-to-mid stage of the negotiation (t=0.36), I will make a careful concession on issues I value less (like issueD and issueE) whilst maintaining high utility on my most critical issues, issueA and issueC, to see if they are responsive to any compromises.

The opponent has rigidly maintained the exact same offer (valueE for all issues) across the entire negotiation, showing no willingness to concede. As we are still in the early-to-mid stage (t=0.38), I will maintain a relatively high utility for myself (around 0.76). I will offer a newly structured compromise that gives them valueE on issueD, while exploring minor concessions on issueB, hoping to find a combination that provokes a counter-offer other than their opening bid.

I am targeting a utility of 0.828, which is appropriate given the relatively early stage of the negotiation (t=0.40). The opponent has been stubbornly repeating the exact same offer (all valueE) without any concessions or messaging. To explore their flexibility, I am offering their requested 'valueE' on my low-weight issue (issueD), while keeping my highest utility values for my most important issues (issueA, issueB, and issueC).

The opponent has held firm on the exact same proposal (valueE for all issues) from the beginning to the current time (t=0.42). To test whether they will show any flexibility, I am strategically conceding to their preferred "valueE" on issueB, while attempting to maintain high utility on my most important issue (issueA) and issueC. If they are evaluating trades, this concession should elicit a counter-offer.

The opponent has finally adjusted their offer by changing issueD from valueE to valueD, indicating they might be exploring compromises or this value holds high utility for them. Since issueD is my least important issue (weight 0.1), I will accept their proposal for issueD (valueD) to encourage further concessions, while returning to my maximum values for all other issues to maintain a high utility of 0.82. We are still early in the negotiation (t=0.44), so maintaining a strong position on my heavily weighted issues is key.

The opponent has held extremely firm for most of the negotiation but just shifted their demand on issueD to valueD. Because issueD has my lowest weight (0.1) and valueD yields low but non-zero utility (0.2), I will accept their valueD for issueD to encourage further concessions. I will also incorporate valueE for issueE (utility 0.38) since it is their target value and also a low-weight issue for me, while keeping my most critical issues (A, B, C) at maximum utility.

The opponent is rigidly repeating the same offer for all issues, with a slight deviation on issueD temporarily before reverting to valueE. At t=0.48, we are nearing the halfway mark. I will start testing combinations that concede my least important issues (such as issueD) to the opponent's preferred 'valueE' while holding firm on my most important issues (issueA, issueB, and issueC) to maintain a relatively high utility.

The opponent has stubbornly insisted on valueE for the majority of issues but recently signaled a preference for valueD on issueD. Knowing that issueD and issueE have lower importance weight (0.1 each), I will concede entirely on these two by matching their latest offer (issueD: valueD, issueE: valueE). To maintain a decent utility of ~0.68 at the midpoint (t=0.5), I will hold my ground on my most heavily weighted issues (issueA and issueB), while offering a moderate compromise on issueC.

The opponent is holding extremely firm, offering almost exactly the same values throughout, primarily pushing for `valueE` across the board, but recently showing a slight flexibility on `issueD` by proposing `valueD`. Since the time is now past halfway (t=0.52), I need to establish a compromise. I will concede fully to their values on `issueD` and `issueE` (which are my lowest-weighted issues anyway) while defending my higher-weighted issues (`issueA`, `issueB`, and `issueC`), slightly softening `issueC` to `valueD` to see if they might prefer it over my previous offers. This keeps my utility at a reasonable ~0.67 while incorporating their latest signal.

The opponent has stubbornly insisted on mostly 'valueE' across the board, but recently shifted slightly to 'valueD' for issueD. Since we are just past the midpoint (t=0.54) and the opponent is heavily holding firm, I will accept their valueD for issueD and valueE for issueE. I will also make a modest concession on issueB (moving to valueB) while holding firm on my most heavily weighted concerns, issueA and issueC, aiming for a utility near 0.69.

The opponent remains very rigid, consistently requesting `valueE` for every issue, which provides me with extremely low utility. I will concede further by providing `valueE` on the lower-weighted issues of D and E, and offering a moderate concession on issue C (shifting to `valueA`). This maintains my utility around 0.69 while moving closer to the opponent's apparent targets, testing if they are willing to accept partial concessions.

The opponent is repeating a nearly uniform offer of `valueE` for all issues, but has occasionally compromised on `issueD` with `valueD`. I will match their concession on `issueD` by offering `valueD` and offer an alternative combination for the remaining issues to test their preferences while maintaining a healthy personal utility around 0.75 since we just passed the midpoint of the negotiation.

The opponent is remaining rigid, insisting on valueE for almost all issues, but has recently shown a willingness to shift to valueD on issueD. To encourage this flexibility, I will concede to their preference on issueD (valueD) and issueE (valueE) since these are my lowest-weighted issues (0.1 each). I will maintain strong values on my highest-weighted issues (A, B, and C) to keep my overall utility above 0.67, specifically trying valueC for issueB to test their response.

I am targeting an overall utility of approximately 0.68 as we pass the 0.6 time fraction. The opponent's last offer showed compromise by changing their ask on `issueC` to `valueD`, while requesting `valueE` for `issueD` and `issueE`. I will concede on these three lower-weight issues by giving the opponent exactly what they just asked for (issueC: valueD, issueD: valueE, issueE: valueE). In return, I will hold firm on `valueA` for my highest-weight issues, `issueA` and `issueB`, protecting my most important utility gains.

The opponent has recently shifted their bids to include `issueC: valueD`, which gives me a reasonable utility of 0.55 for that issue. Since time is at 0.64, I will incorporate this concession on `issueC` while holding firm on my most important issue, `issueA`, and offering a slight concession on `issueB`, `issueD`, and `issueE` to encourage them to meet me on higher-weight matters. My goal is to maintain a high utility overall while demonstrating flexibility on their recent counter-proposals.

The opponent has recently shown flexibility on issue C (shifting to value D) and issue D (shifting to values D and E). I will reciprocate their moves on issues C and D to encourage further concessions, as these align passably with my utility. I will maintain my high-weight issues (issue A and B) at my preferred values, but I will adjust issue E slightly to test if they are receptive to alternative combinations.

The opponent has stubbornly demanded valueE for most issues but recently shifted issueC to valueD and issueD to valueE. Because issues C, D, and E hold lower weights for me to varying degrees, I will concede entirely to their requested values on these three issues. To make this concession sustainable, I am holding firm on my top preference for issueA (my most critical issue) and conceding slightly on issueB.

The time is progressing towards the deadline (t=0.7), so I need to present an offer that gives the opponent more of what they've been requesting while protecting my most critical issues. The opponent has steadfastly demanded 'valueE' for issues C, D, and E, and recently tried asking for 'valueC' on issue A. I will hold absolutely firm on my two highest-weighted issues, A and B, maintaining them at my top utility ('valueA'). In exchange, I am now conceding entirely to their preferred 'valueE' for issues C, D, and E to see if this is enough to secure an agreement.

The opponent has shown interest in valueD for issueC and valueE for issueD and issueE. Given that we are at t=0.72, I will accommodate these preferences on issueC, issueD, and issueE, while holding firm on issueA where my utility is highest. By maintaining a high-utility value on issueA and conceding moderately to valueC on issueB, I target an agreement with a utility close to 0.6, leaving room for further minor concessions closer to the deadline.

The current time fraction is 0.74, so we are in the late stages of negotiation. The opponent has consistently pushed for valueE on most issues, but recently conceded valueD on issueC and issueD. To make progress, I will accept their latest valueD on issueD and their previous concession of valueD on issueC, while keeping issueE at their preferred valueE. To balance this and maintain my overall utility near 0.60, I must hold firm on my most important issue (issueA) at valueA and issueB at valueB.

The opponent remains firm on issueE: valueE and often insists on issueC: valueD/E, and issueD: valueD/E. As the time fraction reaches 0.76, I need to craft an offer that satisfies their specific interests on C, D, and E while securing my high priorities on A and B. I am proposing a variation that grants them their exact request for issueC and issueE, slightly tweaks issueD to valueC, and retains my best values for the heavily weighted issues A and B, yielding a solid ~0.71 utility for me.

The time is currently at t=0.78, placing us in the late stage of the negotiation. The opponent has firmly anchored to 'valueE' for most issues, particularly on issueB where they've made no concessions, while showing minor flexibility on issues A, C, D, and E by dropping to 'valueC' or 'valueD'. I will hold firm on issueA to protect my highest utility issue (importance 0.3), but offer a compromise by moving issueB to valueC and aligning with their preferences by offering valueD on issueC (which they previously proposed) and valueE on issues D and E.

The time fraction is 0.8, meaning the deadline is rapidly approaching. The opponent has frequently requested valueD for issueC and issueD, and valueE for issueE. I will concede to their preference on issueC and issueE, while attempting to retain valueB for issueB, valueC for issueD, and standing fully firm on my most crucial issue, issueA, to secure a utility of roughly 0.64.

The negotiation is entering the late stage (t=0.82), pushing us towards a compromise. The opponent has continuously asked for valueE on most issues but has shown flexibility recently on issueC (offering valueD and valueC) and issueD (offering valueD). I will incorporate their requested concessions on issueC, issueD, and issueE by offering valueD and valueE, while retaining valueA on issueA which is critical to my utility.

The current time fraction is 0.84, bringing us into the late stages of negotiation. The opponent has repeatedly proposed valueD for issueC and consistently demands valueE for issues D and E. I will concede to their latest preferences on issues C, D, and E (valueD, valueE, valueE respectively), while maintaining strong positions on my two most important issues, A and B, targeting a utility near 0.60.

The opponent significantly conceded on issueC by offering valueB (my highest utility value for this issue) and on issueB by offering valueD. Since we are at t=0.86, I will reciprocate this compromise by matching their proposals for issues B, C, D, and E, effectively conceding on four out of five issues. I will hold firm on my top choice for issueA (valueA) to maintain a reasonable overall utility of 0.65 while presenting an offer they are highly likely to accept.

The negotiation is nearing the deadline (t=0.88). The opponent recently indicated a willingness to accept `valueB` for `issueC` and `valueD` for `issueB` (at t=0.86). To find a mutually acceptable compromise, I will hold firm on my most critical issue (`issueA`) while offering the opponent's most heavily requested values for `issueB`, `issueD`, and `issueE` (`valueE` across the board). Since they have shown flexibility on `issueC`, I will incorporate `valueB` for it to balance my own utility.
